buggy_code:
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.boxplot([group1, group2, group3], positions=[1, 2, 3])
ax.boxplot([group4, group5], positions=[4, 5])
task: Plot all 5 groups in a single boxplot call
error_location: lines 2-3: two separate boxplot calls on same axes overlap default styling
clarification_query: How should all 5 groups be combined into one boxplot call?
clarification_options: call boxplot twice, once per set
corrected_code:
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.boxplot([group1, group2, group3, group4, group5], positions=[1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
explanation: Calling boxplot twice on the same axes creates overlapping whisker caps. One call with all groups is the correct approach.
